skill.name: first-responder-program-finder
skill.description: Guides agents through the FirstResponderHomePrograms public website, state pages, program detail pages, and paid workspace navigation.
skill.summary: Find and explain first responder home program information through the public website UI.
skill.author: daanaagua

security_audit.summary: All static command-execution, reconnaissance, and cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown text. The skill contains documentation only and does not include scripts, API calls, credential handling, or prompt-injection text. The only confirmed risk factor is intended navigation to firstresponderhomeprograms.com.

low_findings.title: Intended Public Website Navigation
low_findings.description: The skill directs agents to use firstresponderhomeprograms.com as its primary source. This is expected behavior, but it requires normal web-browsing trust checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in the README and SKILL file as the intended public website. No evidence found of hidden network destinations or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: The static command findings point to Markdown inline code for labels, paths, and UI terms. No executable Ruby, shell, or script files were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation lists and inline Markdown code spans. They do not create command execution or pass user input to a shell.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms In Navigation Documentation
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance flags are caused by words such as finder, search, account, and workspace in website navigation guidance. No host or system probing is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is public website feature navigation. No evidence found of commands or instructions to enumerate local systems, files, users, or networks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cryptography Pattern On Description Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography flag points to the YAML description line. That line describes website program discovery and contains no cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain front matter text. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, credential storage, or cryptographic API usage.

content.value_statement: First responders need clear program information without confusing verified options and research leads. This skill guides agents through the public site and paid workspace boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of the homepage finder with state, role, support type, and repayment filters.
Explains how to read verified matches, under-review signals, and deeper-opportunity teasers.
Directs agents to state pages for statewide coverage and review status.
Directs agents to program pages for rules, support type, repayment terms, and official documents.
Explains when paid workspace and Research Vault features are relevant.
Helps avoid claims that the site approves loans or confirms eligibility.
limitations: Does not make lending, underwriting, or eligibility decisions.
Does not access internal APIs, databases, or source code.
Does not replace direct review of official program documents.
Paid workspace details may depend on the user account and purchase status.

use_cases.title: Check Statewide Options
use_cases.description: Find verified statewide home program matches for a state and first responder role.

use_cases.title: Review Research Leads
use_cases.description: Understand under-review signals and deeper-opportunity teasers without treating them as verified programs.

use_cases.title: Navigate Paid Workspace
use_cases.description: Guide a paid user to saved scenarios, comparisons, conflict checks, and Research Vault records.

prompt_templates.title: Find Programs By State
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the FirstResponderHomePrograms finder for Texas firefighters. Explain verified matches first, then mention under-review signals and deeper opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Explain No Verified Matches
prompt_templates.prompt: The finder shows 0 verified matches for my state and role. Walk me through what to check next and what not to assume.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Free Site Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review how the state page separates verified statewide programs, under-review signals, and deeper-opportunity teasers for Connecticut.

prompt_templates.title: Guide Workspace Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Help a paid user move from a free state overview into the Research Vault, saved programs, comparisons, and conflict checks.

output_examples.output: Start with the homepage finder. Enter Ohio and EMT, then review verified matches before checking under-review signals or deeper opportunities.

output_examples.input: The site shows no verified matches. Does that mean nothing exists?
output_examples.output: No. It means the current filters found no verified statewide match. Clear optional filters, then check under-review signals and deeper opportunities.

output_examples.input: What should a paid user check after choosing a state?
output_examples.output: Open the workspace, load the Research Vault, save relevant records, compare options, review conflict checks, and prepare questions for official sources.

best_practices: Keep verified programs, under-review signals, and deeper research records clearly separated.
Use official program pages and documents before giving detailed rule explanations.
State clearly when paid workspace access is required for deeper local or specialty research.
anti_patterns: Do not describe the site as a lender, quote tool, or underwriting system.
Do not treat under-review or historical records as verified current programs.
Do not claim a user qualifies without direct official source confirmation.

faq.question: Does this skill find mortgage rates?
faq.answer: No. It guides program discovery and research navigation, not mortgage pricing or lender quotes.

faq.question: Can it confirm that I qualify?
faq.answer: No. It can explain public information, but official programs and lenders decide eligibility.

faq.question: What are verified matches?
faq.answer: Verified matches are statewide public program options that the site presents as the free public core.

faq.question: What are under-review signals?
faq.answer: They are official signals that need caution and further review before being treated as usable programs.

faq.question: When is the paid workspace useful?
faq.answer: It is useful for local, specialty, employer, pension, historical, or conditional research beyond the free layer.

faq.question: Does the skill use internal site APIs?
faq.answer: No. The instructions say to use the website UI, state pages, program pages, and workspace pages.
